intro:

However, if you're looking for information on a different plant, I can help you with that. Please provide more context or information about the plant you're looking for.

If you're looking for a similar plant, Ulmus americana, also known as American Elm, has some similar properties. Here's a paragraph about it:

The American Elm (Ulmus americana) is a deciduous tree native to eastern and central North America. The health benefits of American Elm include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of American Elm include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with American Elm include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and fever.
